text.title: Agentic AI Product Development Decision Tree

decision-tree.root-question: Is the AI feature autonomous (acts toward a goal without user trigger) or a copilot (assists user-triggered tasks)?

branch.question: Can a machine-verifiable goal state AND an escalation path be defined right now?

branch.conclusion: Emit the spec using the templates. Write escalation BEFORE happy path; enumerate every autonomous action with its trigger; lock primary metrics to goal-achievement-rate + autonomy-ratio + cost-per-task.
Block. Convert the goal to a concrete predicate (operators + fields + values) before drafting the spec.
Block. Name the human escalation role and on-call rotation before drafting the spec — happy-path-first specs fail to productionize.
Route to `geek/product/product-manager/ai-native-product-development`. Copilots are user-triggered; this methodology only covers autonomous agents.

example.p: Customer-support autonomous agent. Goal predicate `ticket.status == 'resolved' AND csat >= 4` defined. Escalation role `support-lead:alice` named. → conclusion r2-escalation-before-happy-path → emit spec.
